how_it_works.title: How It Works
how_it_works.step1_desc: Upload original image via drag & drop or file selection.
how_it_works.step2_desc: The server performs original proof processing. Nothing is stored on the server after processing.
how_it_works.step3_desc: Download the certified image. Uploading this image again instantly verifies its authenticity. Even a single pixel change is detected.

errors.unsupported_format: Unsupported image format. (PNG, JPG, WebP, BMP, TIFF, GIF supported)
errors.size_limit: File size must be 50MB or less.
errors.server_error: An error occurred during server communication.
errors.empty_file: The file is empty. Please upload a valid image file.
errors.invalid_image: Cannot read the image file. Please upload an uncorrupted PNG, JPG, or WebP file.
errors.image_too_small: The image is too small. Please use an image of at least 300×300 pixels.

items.a: We support most common image formats including PNG, JPG (JPEG), WebP, BMP, TIFF, and GIF. Uploaded images are automatically converted to PNG for certification processing. Maximum file size is 50MB.

items.q: How does the original certification work?
items.a: OriPics uses steganography technology to invisibly embed certification data into the pixel data of your image. When a certified image is uploaded again, the embedded data is extracted to instantly verify its authenticity. Even a single pixel modification is detected.

items.q: Can I share a certified image with others?
items.a: Yes! By creating a Quick Link, anyone can verify the original certification status in their web browser. Quick Links are valid for 7 days. Note: Sending the image file directly through social media (KakaoTalk, Instagram, WhatsApp, etc.) may break the certification, as these platforms automatically compress and resize images. To preserve the certification, we recommend sharing via Quick Link.

items.q: Are my images stored on the server?
items.a: During certification, your image exists only temporarily in server memory and is deleted immediately after processing. Only if you create a Quick Link, the image is stored securely in cloud storage for 7 days before automatic deletion.

No. OriPics converts to lossless PNG format for certification, so there is absolutely no quality degradation. Only imperceptible data is embedded — indistinguishable from the original to the human eye.
